titleStmt.title: SII 31.6: original edition by C. A. Padmanabha Sastri and T. S. Ravishankar
No. 6. (A.R. No. B 283 of 1938.) TIPPALŇ™RU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a Slab lying under a tree to the south of the ruined temple of Vńęrabhadra.
This record can be assigned to the 7th century A.D. on the basis of palaeography as well as internal evidences.

Registers the grant of fifty marturu of land as pannasa situated in the village TippalŇęru to KiŠłĽvŇęru KattiŇõarmma, a (pńĀradńĀya) brńĀhmin of BhńĀradvńĀja gŇćtra belonging to TarkkapuŠłĽŇćla probably by the king EŠĻüikallu MuturńĀju PuŠĻáyakumńĀruŠĻČŠĻüu at the time of BŠĻõihaspatihŇćra, on the second tithi of the dark fortnight (Chńękubidiya) of the month of KńĀrtńęka, Monday, when the nakshatra of the day was PunarupŇęshaŠĻĀbu (punarvasu). The donor king is endowed with titles such as GaŠĻáyamńĀnuŠĻČŠĻüu, MaŠĻüuntra-piŠłćugu, MadamuditunŠĻüru, UttamŇćttńĀmunŠĻüu and is said to have been ruling the RńďnaŠłću country from the capital Cherpali. The record ends with a statement of ChńĀmana kńĀlu dharmmam, which indicates the protector of this dharma is ChńĀmanakńĀlu.
(Pub. Epigraphia Indica. Vol. XXVII, pp 231 ff. Ins. No. F.)

encodingDesc.p: Encoded in TEI/EpiDoc according to the DHARMA Encoding Guide for Documentary Editions, version 2

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

svastiŇõrńę . Erikala muttu
rńĀju puŠĻĀŠĻáyakumńĀrunŠĻüu gaŠĻáya
mńĀnunŠĻüu maŠĻüantrapiŠłćugu madamu
ditunŠĻüuttayŇćttamunŠĻüayina vńĀŠĻĀ
nŠĻüu cepaliya paŠĻ≠ukńĀnu rńďnńĀ ŠĻádńď
Šł∑ucu tarkkapuŠł∑Ňćla pńĀradńĀya
ki ŠĻüevuru kattiŇõarmmaku tirpavńĀ
rapanńĀra koŠĻáŠłćakńĀrttiya cńęku
ra biŠłćiya sŇćmavńĀraŠĻĀbuŠĻĀ puŠĻáyaru
pupyaŠĻĀbu bŠĻõhaspati hŇćrakńĀ
nu Embhadiye cńĀmaŠĻáakńĀla dharmaŠĻĀ
